GRIEVANCE REDRESSAL COMMITTEE (GRC)
The Grievance Redressal Committee is established at Sri Eshwar to provide a platform for redressal of genuine grievances of students and staff. We believe that all grievances must be heard and addressed in the appropriate manner.
According to UGC, AICTE and Anna University regulations, the objectives, Mode of Grievance Redressal and the composition of the GRC Committee including faculty and student members for the academic year are given below.
OBJECTIVES
The Grievance Cell has been established with the aim of fostering a healthy and harmonious environment for both faculty and students, as well as addressing concerns from parents.
Promoting an environment in which students feel comfortable expressing their concerns and issues freely, without the fear of facing any form of retaliation.
Ensuring that student grievances are addressed effectively through an impartial and unbiased process, thereby facilitating fair resolutions.
List of Anti-ragging Committee MembersList of Anti-ragging Vigilance Squad Members.
MODES OF GRIEVANCE REDRESSAL
Drop a letter in Grievance/Suggestion Box available in front of the office.
Mail to
grc@sece.ac.in
Use online Grievance module through Impress ERP using My performance module.
Reach out to GRC members through their Mobile numbers.
